description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent red gable roof and white exterior walls. The front facade includes a central entrance flanked by large windows and a small covered porch area. The side elevations reveal additional windows and a protruding section, likely a sunroom or bay area, enhancing the architectural interest. The rear of the bungalow is simpler, with fewer windows and a solid wall, while the roof extends over the main living space and side extension. The building sits on a rectangular concrete foundation surrounded by a green lawn, typical of suburban or residential neighborhoods. The overall design is compact, functional, and inviting, with a focus on symmetry and accessibility.
description: A single-story white bungalow with a red gable roof, central entrance, and a small front porch, surrounded by a green lawn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large windows, and the covered porch area.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the protruding sunroom or bay area and additional windows.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simpler facade, fewer windows, and the back of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wall and the extension of the roof over the side section.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
